title: Undo Last Commit
description: You made a commit you want to take back. Maybe the message was wrong, or you committed the wrong files.

steps.explanation: Moves HEAD back one commit. Your files are unchanged and staged, ready to re-commit.
Moves HEAD back and DELETES the changes.
steps.warning: This permanently deletes your changes. There is no undo.

title: Amend the Last Commit Message
description: You made a typo in your last commit message or need to change it.

steps.explanation: Replaces the last commit with a new one that has the updated message. Do not do this if you have already pushed!

title: Add a Forgotten File to the Last Commit
description: You made a commit but forgot to include a file.

steps.explanation: Adds the forgotten file to the staging area, then amends the previous commit without changing its message. Do not do this if you already pushed.

title: Undo Multiple Commits
description: You want to undo the last N commits.

steps.explanation: Moves HEAD back N commits. Your files are unchanged and staged.
Moves HEAD back N commits and DELETES all changes.
steps.warning: This permanently deletes your changes. There is no undo.

title: Committed to Wrong Branch
description: You accidentally committed your changes to the wrong branch (e.g. main instead of a feature branch).

steps.explanation: Creates a new branch at your current commit. Then moves the current branch back one commit (removing it from the wrong branch). Finally, checks out the new branch with your work intact.

title: Committed Sensitive Data
description: You accidentally committed a file containing a password, API key, or other sensitive information.

steps.condition: It was the very last commit
steps.explanation: Removes the file from git tracking, then amends the last commit to exclude it.

steps.condition: It is deep in your history
steps.explanation: Uses git filter-repo (requires installation) to completely rewrite history without the file.
steps.warning: This is extremely destructive. You will rewrite history for the entire repo. Anyone else working on the project will need to re-clone.

title: Delete a Local Branch
description: You want to delete a local branch that you no longer need.

steps.explanation: Safely deletes the branch only if it has already been merged into your current branch.
Forces the deletion of the branch even if it has unmerged changes.
steps.warning: You will lose any unmerged commits on this branch.

title: Delete a Remote Branch
description: You want to delete a branch on the remote server (e.g. GitHub).

steps.explanation: Tells the remote server to delete the specified branch.

title: Rename a Branch
description: You want to rename your current local branch.

steps.explanation: Renames the branch you are currently on.

title: Recover a Deleted Branch
description: You accidentally deleted a branch and need it back.

steps.explanation: Use reflog to find the commit hash where the branch was before deletion. Then create a new branch pointing to that hash.

title: Switch Branches with Uncommitted Changes
description: You want to switch branches but Git won't let you because you have uncommitted changes.

steps.explanation: Stashes your changes temporarily, switches branches, and then applies your stashed changes to the new branch.

title: Create a Branch from an Old Commit
description: You want to create a new branch starting from a specific point in the past.

steps.explanation: Creates a new branch and checks it out, starting at the specified commit.

title: Undo a Push (Rewrite History)
description: You pushed commits to the remote that you want to undo.

steps.condition: You haven't shared the branch with anyone else
steps.explanation: Resets your local branch back one commit and force pushes to overwrite the remote.
steps.warning: Never do this on a shared branch (like main)! It will ruin history for other developers.

steps.explanation: Creates a NEW commit that does the exact opposite of the bad commit, keeping history intact for everyone.

title: Force Push Safely
description: You need to force push (e.g. after a rebase), but want to make sure you don't overwrite a teammate's work.

steps.explanation: Forces the push, but will abort if the remote branch has new commits that you haven't fetched yet. Much safer than --force.

title: Pull When You Have Local Changes
description: You want to pull new changes from remote, but you have uncommitted changes that might conflict.

steps.explanation: Hides your changes, pulls the new code, then puts your changes back on top.

title: Fix a Rejected Push
description: Git says 'Updates were rejected because the remote contains work that you do not have locally.'

steps.explanation: Pulls the remote changes and replays your local commits on top of them, avoiding a messy merge commit.

title: Change the Remote URL
description: You need to point your local repository to a different remote URL (e.g. moved from GitHub to GitLab).

steps.explanation: Changes the URL for the remote named 'origin'.

title: Undo a Merge
description: You completed a merge but realize it was a mistake and want to undo it.

steps.explanation: Assuming the merge was your very last commit, this will remove the merge commit and restore your branch to how it was before.

steps.condition: You already pushed to a shared branch
steps.explanation: Reverts the merge commit safely. The '-m 1' tells Git to keep the main branch's history and revert the incoming branch's changes.

title: Abort a Merge in Progress
description: You are in the middle of a merge (perhaps with conflicts) and just want to give up and go back to how things were.

steps.explanation: Stops the merge and restores your files to their state before you ran 'git merge'.

title: Resolve a Merge Conflict
description: Git stopped a merge because of conflicts and you need to fix them.

commands: # Edit the conflicted files in your editor
steps.explanation: Check git status to see which files are conflicted. Open them, look for the <<< === >>> markers, choose the correct code, add the files, and commit.

title: Abort a Rebase
description: You started a rebase, got overwhelmed by conflicts, and want to cancel it.

steps.explanation: Cancels the rebase entirely and puts your branch back exactly how it was.

title: Undo a Rebase
description: You finished a rebase, but it messed things up and you want to put your branch back to how it was before you rebased.

steps.explanation: Look at the reflog to find the state of HEAD right before the rebase started. Then reset hard to that point.

title: Unstage a File (Keep Changes)
description: You ran 'git add' on a file by accident and want to remove it from the staging area.

steps.explanation: Removes the file from the staging area. Your actual modifications to the file are kept perfectly safe.

title: Discard Changes to a File
description: You made changes to a file but want to throw them away and go back to the last committed version.

steps.explanation: Overwrites the file with the version from your last commit.
steps.warning: This permanently deletes your uncommitted changes to this file.

title: Recover a Deleted File
description: You deleted a file and realized you still need it.

steps.condition: You haven't committed the deletion yet
steps.explanation: Brings the file back from your last commit.
Brings the file back from the commit before your current one.

title: Remove a File from History Entirely
description: You committed a large file or sensitive data a long time ago and need to eradicate it completely from all past commits.

steps.explanation: Uses the git-filter-repo tool to scrub the file from all commits.
steps.warning: This rewrites history for your entire repository. Other developers will need to re-clone.

title: Find When a Bug Was Introduced
description: Something is broken, and you need to find exactly which past commit introduced the bug.

commands: # Git checks out a middle commit. Test your app.
# Run 'git bisect good' or 'git bisect bad'
# Repeat until Git finds the exact commit.
steps.explanation: Starts a binary search through your commit history. You tell Git if a commit is good or bad, and it quickly narrows down the culprit.

title: Stash Uncommitted Changes
description: You have changes you aren't ready to commit, but you need a clean working directory (e.g. to pull or switch branches).

steps.explanation: Saves your uncommitted changes to a temporary shelf and reverts your working directory to match HEAD.

title: Recover a Dropped Stash
description: You ran 'git stash drop' or 'git stash clear' and realize you needed that work.

steps.explanation: Finds all dangling commits (which includes dropped stashes), lets you inspect them to find yours, and then applies it.

title: I made a typo in my last commit message
description: I made a typo in my last commit message

commands: git commit --amend -m "Your corrected commit message here"
steps.explanation: Changes only the commit message of the most recent commit. Safe only if not yet pushed.
steps.warning: Do not use on commits that have already been pushed to a shared branch.

title: I forgot to add a file in my last commit
description: I forgot to add a file in my last commit

steps.explanation: Adds the missing file to the last commit without changing the message.

title: I accidentally deleted a file
description: I accidentally deleted a file

steps.explanation: Restores the file from the last commit (Git 2.23+). For older Git: git checkout -- path/to/file

title: I committed changes to the wrong branch
description: I committed changes to the wrong branch

steps.explanation: Copies the commit to the right branch. Then go back and reset the wrong branch if needed: git reset --hard HEAD~1
steps.warning: Be very careful when resetting shared branches.

title: I staged the wrong files by mistake
description: I staged the wrong files by mistake

steps.explanation: Unstages the file but keeps changes in working directory (Git 2.23+). Older: git reset HEAD path/to/file

title: Undo last commit but keep my changes
description: Undo last commit but keep my changes

steps.explanation: Removes the commit but leaves all changes staged and ready to recommit.

title: Completely undo last commit and discard changes
description: Completely undo last commit and discard changes

steps.explanation: Deletes the commit and all changes made in it.
steps.warning: This permanently loses uncommitted changes! Use carefully.

title: I'm in detached HEAD state
description: I'm in detached HEAD state

steps.explanation: Creates a new branch from your current position so you can continue working normally.

title: I have merge conflicts after pulling
description: I have merge conflicts after pulling

commands: Edit the conflicted files (look for ======= markers), then: git add <file> && git commit
steps.explanation: Manually resolve conflicts in the files, stage them, and complete the merge.

title: I committed and pushed a secret or API key
description: I committed and pushed a secret or API key

commands: Use git filter-repo or BFG Repo-Cleaner to remove it, then force push.
steps.explanation: Immediately change the leaked secret on the service side too!
steps.warning: Force pushing rewrites history – coordinate with your team.

title: I want to rename my current local branch
description: I want to rename my current local branch

steps.explanation: Renames the currently checked-out branch.

title: Discard all my uncommitted changes
description: Discard all my uncommitted changes

steps.explanation: Resets working directory and removes untracked files.
steps.warning: This deletes everything not committed – no undo!

title: I messed up a rebase or merge and want to go back
description: I messed up a rebase or merge and want to go back

steps.explanation: Reflog shows your recent Git actions. Find the good state and reset to it.
steps.warning: This discards changes completely.

title: I made commits directly on main that should be on a new feature branch
description: I made commits directly on main that should be on a new feature branch

commands: git checkout -b feature-branch && git checkout main && git reset --hard origin/main
steps.explanation: Moves your commits to a new branch and resets main to the remote state.
steps.warning: Only do this if main has not been pushed with your changes.

title: I accidentally committed and pushed large files (videos, binaries, etc.)
description: I accidentally committed and pushed large files (videos, binaries, etc.)

steps.explanation: Removes large files from history. Use BFG Repo-Cleaner as an alternative for simpler cases.
steps.warning: Force pushing rewrites history – inform your team first and change any affected links.

title: I forgot to add .gitignore and committed build files / node_modules / .env
description: I forgot to add .gitignore and committed build files / node_modules / .env

commands: Add patterns to .gitignore, then: git rm -r --cached node_modules && git commit -m "Remove ignored files"
steps.explanation: Stops tracking the files while keeping them locally.

title: I pushed my changes to the wrong remote branch
description: I pushed my changes to the wrong remote branch

commands: git push origin --delete wrong-branch-name && git push origin correct-branch
steps.explanation: Delete the wrong remote branch (if safe) and push to the correct one. Use git push --force-with-lease for safety on updated branches.

title: My rebase went wrong and I want to cancel it
description: My rebase went wrong and I want to cancel it

steps.explanation: Returns your branch to the state before the rebase started.

title: I rebased my branch and now push is rejected
description: I rebased my branch and now push is rejected

steps.explanation: Safely overwrites the remote branch after rebase (safer than plain --force).
steps.warning: Never force push to shared branches like main without team coordination.

title: I committed irrelevant or temporary changes (like debug logs)
description: I committed irrelevant or temporary changes (like debug logs)

steps.explanation: Undo the commit, then selectively stage only the good changes.

title: I did a hard reset and lost my commits
description: I did a hard reset and lost my commits

steps.explanation: Reflog shows your recent actions. Find the commit before the bad reset and restore it.
steps.warning: This discards changes completely.

title: git pull created unwanted merge commits or conflicts
description: git pull created unwanted merge commits or conflicts

steps.explanation: Next time, use --rebase to keep a linear history instead of merge commits.

title: I'm stuck in the middle of a merge
description: I'm stuck in the middle of a merge

steps.explanation: Cancels the merge and returns to the previous state.

title: fatal: remote origin already exists (when adding remote)
description: fatal: remote origin already exists (when adding remote)

steps.explanation: Updates the existing remote URL instead of trying to add a duplicate.

title: I made several commits directly on main that should be on a feature branch
description: I made several commits directly on main that should be on a feature branch

commands: git checkout -b feature-branch && git checkout main && git reset --hard origin/main
steps.explanation: Moves your work to a new branch and resets main to the clean remote state.
steps.warning: Only safe if you haven't pushed the commits to main yet.

title: I accidentally deleted a Git tag
description: I accidentally deleted a Git tag

steps.explanation: Recreate the tag using the commit hash from reflog.

title: I have multiple stashes and want to apply only one
description: I have multiple stashes and want to apply only one

commands: git stash list && git stash apply stash@{1}
steps.explanation: List stashes first, then apply the desired one by index.

title: Push rejected: Updates were rejected because the remote contains work you do not have locally
description: Push rejected: Updates were rejected because the remote contains work you do not have locally

steps.explanation: Fetch remote changes, rebase your work on top, then push cleanly.

title: My commit history has many poor or unclear messages
description: My commit history has many poor or unclear messages

commands: git rebase -i HEAD~n (then change 'pick' to 'reword' or 'squash')
steps.explanation: Interactive rebase lets you edit, combine, or rewrite recent commits.

title: I ran git clean and deleted untracked files I wanted to keep
description: I ran git clean and deleted untracked files I wanted to keep

commands: Check your recycle bin / backup. Prevention: always use git clean -n first to preview.
steps.explanation: git clean is destructive – always dry-run with -n or -d first.

title: I checked out a commit directly and now I'm in detached HEAD
description: I checked out a commit directly and now I'm in detached HEAD

steps.explanation: Create a new branch to save your current work safely.

title: I want to undo a commit that has already been pushed
description: I want to undo a commit that has already been pushed

steps.explanation: Creates a new commit that undoes the changes without rewriting history (safe for shared branches).

title: My Git submodule is not updating or shows wrong version
description: My Git submodule is not updating or shows wrong version

commands: git submodule update --init --recursive && git submodule foreach git pull
steps.explanation: Initialize and update submodules to the correct commit.

title: I don't remember what I did but my branch is messed up
description: I don't remember what I did but my branch is messed up

steps.explanation: Reflog is your Git history of actions – use it to find any previous state and reset or checkout from there.

title: GitHub rejects my push because of a large file (>100MB)
description: GitHub rejects my push because of a large file (>100MB)

steps.explanation: Remove the large file from history using git filter-repo (or BFG Repo-Cleaner). Add it to .gitignore afterwards.
steps.warning: Force pushing rewrites history – coordinate with your team.

title: Files are still being tracked even after adding to .gitignore
description: Files are still being tracked even after adding to .gitignore

commands: git rm -r --cached path/to/folder && git commit -m "Stop tracking ignored files"
steps.explanation: Removes files from Git index while keeping them on your disk.

title: I accidentally committed node_modules or vendor folder
description: I accidentally committed node_modules or vendor folder

commands: git rm -r --cached node_modules && git commit -m "Remove node_modules from tracking"
steps.explanation: Add node_modules/ to .gitignore first, then untrack it.

title: I made commits directly on main/main that should be on a feature branch
description: I made commits directly on main/main that should be on a feature branch

commands: git checkout -b feature-branch && git checkout main && git reset --hard origin/main
steps.explanation: Moves your commits to a new branch and resets main to the remote state.
steps.warning: Only safe if you haven't pushed yet.

title: I rebased a shared/public branch and now everything is broken
description: I rebased a shared/public branch and now everything is broken

commands: git reset --hard origin/branch-name (or use reflog to recover)
steps.explanation: Avoid rebasing shared branches. Use merge instead for public branches.
steps.warning: Rebasing public branches causes problems for teammates.

title: I used git push --force and overwrote my teammate's work
description: I used git push --force and overwrote my teammate's work

commands: git reflog && git reset --hard <good-commit-hash> && git push --force-with-lease
steps.explanation: Use --force-with-lease instead of --force for safer overwrites.
steps.warning: Always communicate before force pushing shared branches.

title: git stash pop caused conflicts
description: git stash pop caused conflicts

commands: Resolve conflicts manually, then git add <file> && git stash drop
steps.explanation: Stash conflicts are resolved like merge conflicts.

title: I have many stashes and forgot which is which
description: I have many stashes and forgot which is which

steps.explanation: Shows all stashes with messages and indices (stash@{0}, stash@{1}, etc.).

title: Apply only one specific stash without dropping others
description: Apply only one specific stash without dropping others

steps.explanation: Apply without removing from stash list. Use git stash pop to apply and drop.

title: I lost uncommitted changes after switching branches
description: I lost uncommitted changes after switching branches

steps.explanation: Reflog can help recover recent changes even if not stashed.

title: I committed debug prints or temporary code
description: I committed debug prints or temporary code

commands: git reset --soft HEAD~1 && git restore --staged unwanted-file
steps.explanation: Undo commit, unstage bad files, then recommit only good changes.

title: I made an empty commit by mistake
description: I made an empty commit by mistake

steps.explanation: Removes the empty commit while keeping any staged changes.

title: I renamed a local branch but the remote still has the old name
description: I renamed a local branch but the remote still has the old name

commands: git push origin --delete old-branch && git push origin new-branch
steps.explanation: Delete old remote branch and push the new one.

title: Git submodule shows wrong version or not updating
description: Git submodule shows wrong version or not updating

steps.explanation: Initialize and update submodules to the recorded commit.

title: I moved or renamed a submodule and it's broken
description: I moved or renamed a submodule and it's broken

commands: git submodule deinit -f path && git rm -f path && git submodule add <url> new/path
steps.explanation: Properly remove and re-add the submodule at the new location.

title: git pull is taking forever or failing
description: git pull is taking forever or failing

steps.explanation: Fetch first, then rebase for cleaner history.

title: I checked out a tag and now I'm in detached HEAD
description: I checked out a tag and now I'm in detached HEAD

steps.explanation: Create a branch to make changes safely from the tag.

title: Undo a specific commit that was already pushed
description: Undo a specific commit that was already pushed

steps.explanation: Creates a new commit that reverses the changes (safe for shared history).

title: I want to combine my last N commits into one
description: I want to combine my last N commits into one

steps.explanation: Interactive rebase for cleaning up recent commit history.

title: Change the message of an older unpushed commit
description: Change the message of an older unpushed commit

steps.explanation: Allows editing commit messages during interactive rebase.

title: I ran git clean and deleted important untracked files
description: I ran git clean and deleted important untracked files

commands: Check recycle bin / backups. Always run git clean -n first to preview.
steps.explanation: git clean -fd is destructive – use dry-run (-n) option.

title: My remote URL is wrong or points to the wrong repository
description: My remote URL is wrong or points to the wrong repository

steps.explanation: Updates the remote without removing and re-adding.

title: Remote branches I deleted locally still show up
description: Remote branches I deleted locally still show up

steps.explanation: Removes references to deleted remote branches.

title: My branch history is full of unnecessary merge commits
description: My branch history is full of unnecessary merge commits

steps.explanation: Rebase keeps history linear instead of creating merge commits.

title: Cherry-pick created conflicts
description: Cherry-pick created conflicts

steps.explanation: Continue or abort with git cherry-pick --abort if needed.

title: I deleted a branch locally and remotely by mistake
description: I deleted a branch locally and remotely by mistake

commands: git reflog && git branch branch-name <hash> && git push origin branch-name
steps.explanation: Recover using reflog then push again.

title: git status is very slow on a large repository
description: git status is very slow on a large repository

steps.explanation: Clean up and optimize the repository.

title: I committed .env or secrets file
description: I committed .env or secrets file

commands: git rm --cached .env && git commit -m "Remove .env from tracking" && add to .gitignore
steps.explanation: Immediately rotate any exposed secrets.
steps.warning: Change passwords/keys after removal.

title: I pushed to my fork instead of upstream
description: I pushed to my fork instead of upstream

commands: git remote add upstream <original-url> && git push upstream branch
steps.explanation: Set up upstream remote for the original repository.

title: I need to amend a commit that was already pushed
description: I need to amend a commit that was already pushed

steps.explanation: Only do this on your own branches.
steps.warning: Rewrites history – inform teammates.

title: I did git add . and added everything including junk
description: I did git add . and added everything including junk

steps.explanation: Unstage everything, then add only what you want.

title: I created a branch with a typo in the name
description: I created a branch with a typo in the name

steps.explanation: Rename the current branch. For remote: delete and push new.

title: My PR has conflicts when trying to merge
description: My PR has conflicts when trying to merge

commands: git checkout main && git pull && git checkout my-branch && git rebase main
steps.explanation: Rebase your branch on latest main to resolve conflicts locally.

title: Large files are still stored in Git instead of LFS
description: Large files are still stored in Git instead of LFS

commands: git lfs install && git lfs track "*.psd" && git add .gitattributes
steps.explanation: Set up Git LFS for large binary files.

title: I'm on a shallow clone and can't access full history
description: I'm on a shallow clone and can't access full history

steps.explanation: Convert shallow clone to full clone.

title: I need to find which commit introduced a bug
description: I need to find which commit introduced a bug

commands: git bisect start && git bisect bad && git bisect good <good-commit>
steps.explanation: Binary search through history to find the bad commit.

title: git tag fails because tag already exists
description: git tag fails because tag already exists

steps.explanation: Force overwrite existing tag (careful with pushed tags).

title: I did git reset --hard and lost important changes
description: I did git reset --hard and lost important changes

steps.explanation: Reflog acts as a safety net for recent actions.
steps.warning: This discards changes completely.

title: My commit messages are unclear like "fix" or "update"
description: My commit messages are unclear like "fix" or "update"

commands: git rebase -i HEAD~N && reword the messages
steps.explanation: Rewrite recent messages for better history.

title: I want to ignore files only on my machine (not for team)
description: I want to ignore files only on my machine (not for team)

steps.explanation: Keeps ignores local without committing .gitignore changes.

title: I'm stuck in a merge state I don't want
description: I'm stuck in a merge state I don't want

steps.explanation: Cancels the merge and returns to pre-merge state.

title: My interactive rebase is messed up
description: My interactive rebase is messed up

steps.explanation: Returns branch to state before rebase.

title: I always have to type the full git push origin branch-name
description: I always have to type the full git push origin branch-name

steps.explanation: Sets upstream so future pushes are just git push.

title: I committed dist/, build/, or target/ folder
description: I committed dist/, build/, or target/ folder

commands: git rm -r --cached dist && git commit -m "Remove build artifacts"
steps.explanation: Add dist/ to .gitignore.

title: I want to see even older actions in reflog
description: I want to see even older actions in reflog

steps.explanation: Reflog expires after ~90 days by default.

title: I want to commit a file that is ignored by .gitignore
description: I want to commit a file that is ignored by .gitignore

steps.explanation: Overrides ignore rules for that specific add.

title: Too many stale remote branches cluttering my repo
description: Too many stale remote branches cluttering my repo

commands: git fetch --prune && git remote prune origin
steps.explanation: Cleans up deleted remote branches locally.

title: I want to work on two branches at the same time
description: I want to work on two branches at the same time

steps.explanation: Git worktrees allow multiple checkouts in one repo.

title: My commits are not showing as verified (GPG signing)
description: My commits are not showing as verified (GPG signing)

commands: git config --global commit.gpgsign true && git commit -S
steps.explanation: Set up GPG signing for commits.

title: Find who last changed a specific line of code
description: Find who last changed a specific line of code

steps.explanation: Shows author and commit for each line (or range).
